siunitx (MathJax Node)

ang

LaTeX SVG ClearSpeak 
\ang{10}
 10 degrees 
\ang{12.3}
 12.3 degrees 
\ang{4,5}
 4.5 degrees 
\ang{1;2;3}
 1 degree 2 degree-minutes 3 degree-seconds 
\ang{;;1}
 1 degree-second 
\ang{+10;;}
 10 degrees 
\ang{-0;1;}
 0 degrees 1 degree-minute 
\ang{2.67}
 2.67 degrees 
\ang{2;3;4}
 2 degrees 3 degree-minutes 4 degree-seconds 
\ang[angle-mode = arc]{2.67}
 2.67 degrees 
\ang[angle-mode = arc]{2;3;4}
 2 degrees 3 degree-minutes 4 degree-seconds 
\ang[angle-mode = decimal]{2.67}
 2.67 degrees 
\ang[angle-mode = decimal]{2;3;4}
 2.051 111 111 111 111 degrees 
\ang{2.67}
 2.67 degrees 
\ang[number-angle-product = {\,}]{2.67}
 2.67 degrees 
\ang{6;7;6.5}
 6 degrees 7 degree-minutes 6.5 degree-seconds 
\ang[angle-separator = {\,}]{6;7;6.5}
 6 degrees 7 degree-minutes 6.5 degree-seconds 
\ang[angle-separator = {\,}]{6;;}
 6 degrees 
\ang[fill-angle-degrees]{;-2;}
 negative 0 degrees 2 degree-minutes 
\sisetup{fill-angle-degrees}\ang{;-2;}
 negative 0 degrees 2 degree-minutes 
\displaylines{
  \ang{-1;;} \\
  \ang{;-2;} \\
  \ang{;;-3} \\
}
 3 lines Line 1: negative 1 degrees Line 2: negative 0 degrees 2 degree-minutes Line 3: negative 0 degrees 3 degree-seconds 
\displaylines{
  \sisetup{fill-angle-degrees}
  \ang{-1;;} \\
  \ang{;-2;} \\
  \ang{;;-3} \\
  \sisetup{fill-angle-degrees = false}
}
 3 lines Line 1: negative 1 degrees Line 2: negative 0 degrees 2 degree-minutes Line 3: negative 0 degrees 3 degree-seconds 
\displaylines{
  \sisetup{fill-angle-minutes}
  \ang{-1;;} \\
  \ang{;-2;} \\
  \ang{;;-3} \\
  \sisetup{fill-angle-minutes = false}
}
 3 lines Line 1: negative 1 degrees 0 degree-minutes Line 2: negative 2 degree-minutes Line 3: negative 0 degree-minutes 3 degree-seconds 
\displaylines{
  \sisetup{fill-angle-seconds}
  \ang{-1;;} \\
  \ang{;-2;} \\
  \ang{;;-3} \\
  \sisetup{fill-angle-seconds = false}
}
 3 lines Line 1: negative 1 degrees 0 degree-seconds Line 2: negative 2 degree-minutes 0 degree-seconds Line 3: negative 3 degree-seconds 
\ang{6;7;6.5}
 6 degrees 7 degree-minutes 6.5 degree-seconds 
\sisetup{
	angle-symbol-degree = d ,
	angle-symbol-minute = m ,
	angle-symbol-second = s
}
\ang{6;7;6.5}
\sisetup{
	angle-symbol-degree = \degree ,
	angle-symbol-minute = ' ,
	angle-symbol-second = ''
}
 6 normal d 7 meters 6.5 seconds 
\ang{45.697}
 45.697 degrees 
\ang{6;7;6.5}
 6 degrees 7 degree-minutes 6.5 degree-seconds 
\ang[angle-symbol-over-decimal]{45.697}
 45 period degrees 697 
\ang[angle-symbol-over-decimal]{6;7;6.5}
 6 degrees 7 degree-minutes 6 period with degree-seconds above 5

num

LaTeX SVG ClearSpeak 
\num{0}
 0 
\num{1234}
 1234 
\num{1234p}
 
\num{12345}
 12 345 
\num{0.123}
 0.123 
\num{0.10000}
 0.100 00 
\num{.123}
 0.123 
\num{0,1234}
 0.1234 
\num{.12345}
 0.123 45 
\num{3.45d-4}
 3.45 times 10 to the negative 4 power 
\num{-e10}
 negative 0 10 to the tenth power 
\num{< 10}
 is less than 10 
\num{>> 5}
 is much greater than 5 
\num{\le 0.12}
 is less than or equal to 0.12 
\num{9.99(9)}
 9.99 times 9 
\num{9.99 +- 0.09}
 9.99 times 9 
\num{9.99 \pm 0.09}
 9.99 times 9 
\num{123 +- 4.5}
 123.0 times 45 
\num{12.3 +- 6}
 12.3 times 6 
\num{123.4(12)}
 123.4 times 12 
\num{123.4(1.2)}
 123.4 times 12 
\num{123.4(12)(45)}
 123.4 times 12 times 45 
\num{123.4 \pm 1.2 \pm 4.5}
 123.4 times 12 times 45 
\num[uncertainty-mode=separate]{123.4(12)(45)}
 123.4 plus or minus 1.2 plus or minus 4.5 
\num[uncertainty-mode=separate]{123.4 \pm 1.2 \pm 4.5}
 123.4 plus or minus 1.2 plus or minus 4.5 
\num{\sqrt{2}}
 
\num[parse-numbers = false]{\sqrt{2}}
 the square root of 2

 14 
\num[evaluate-expression, expression = 10 * (#1)]{2 + 4 * 3}
 140

\num[retain-negative-zero]{-0}

\num[exponent-mode = fixed, fixed-exponent=0]{1.00e-2}

 12 300 period 
\displaylines{
  \sisetup{exponent-mode = threshold}
  \num{0.001} \\
  \num{0.012} \\
  \num{0.123} \\
  \num{1} \\
  \num{12} \\
  \num{123} \\
  \num{1234} \\
  \sisetup{exponent-mode = input}
}
 7 lines Line 1: 1 times 10 to the negative 3 power Line 2: 0.012 Line 3: 0.123 Line 4: 1 Line 5: 12 Line 6: 123 Line 7: 1.234 times 10 cubed 
\displaylines{
  \sisetup{exponent-mode = threshold, exponent-thresholds = -2:2 }
  \num{0.001} \\
  \num{0.012} \\
  \num{0.123} \\
  \num{1} \\
  \num{12} \\
  \num{123} \\
  \num{1234} \\
  \sisetup{exponent-mode = input}
}
 7 lines Line 1: 1 times 10 to the negative 3 power Line 2: 1.2 times 10 to the negative 2 power Line 3: 0.123 Line 4: 1 Line 5: 12 Line 6: 1.23 times 10 squared Line 7: 1.234 times 10 cubed

complexnum & complexqty

LaTeX SVG ClearSpeak 
\complexnum{1 + i}
 1 plus normal i 
\complexnum{1:45}
 1 angle 45 degrees 
\complexnum{2i}
 0 2 normal i 
\complexnum{2}
 2 
\complexnum[complex-mode = cartesian]{1 + i}
 1 plus normal i 
\complexnum[complex-mode = cartesian, round-mode = places]{1:45}
 0.71 plus 0.71 normal i 
\complexnum[complex-mode = polar, round-mode = places, round-precision = 3, round-pad = false]{1 + 1i}
 1.414 angle 45 degrees 
\complexnum[complex-mode = polar]{1:45}
 1 angle 45 degrees 
\complexnum{9.99 + 88.8i}
 9.99 plus 88.8 normal i 
\complexnum{9.99 + i88.8}
 9.99 plus 88.8 normal i 
\complexnum[output-complex-root = i]{1+2i}
 1 plus 2 i 
\complexnum[output-complex-root = j]{1+2i}
 1 plus 2 j 
\complexnum{67-0.9i}
 67 minus 0.9 normal i 
\complexnum[complex-root-position = before-number]{67-0.9i}
 67 minus normal i 0.9 
\complexnum[complex-root-position = after-number]{67-0.9i}
 67 minus 0.9 normal i 
\complexqty{1:1}{\ohm}
 1 angle 1 degrees empty normal ohm 
\complexqty[complex-angle-unit = radians]{1:1}{\ohm}
 1 angle 1 empty normal ohm 
\complexqty[complex-symbol-angle = \mathrm{A}]{1:1}{\ohm}
 1 ampere 1 degrees empty normal ohm 
\complexqty[complex-symbol-degree = d]{1:1}{\ohm}
 1 angle 1 d empty normal ohm 
\complexqty{1i}{\ohm}
 0 normal i empty normal ohm 
\complexqty[print-complex-unity]{1i}{\ohm}
 0 1 normal i empty normal ohm

unit

LaTeX SVG ClearSpeak 
\unit{kg.m/s^2}
 kilograms meters divided by seconds to the negative 2 power 
\unit{g_{polymer}~mol_{cat}.s^{-1}}
 grams sub polymer meters sub cat per second 
\unit{\kilo\gram\metre\per\square\second}
 kilograms meters seconds to the negative 2 power 
\unit{\gram\per\cubic\centi\metre}
 grams centimeters to the negative 3 power 
\unit{\square\volt\cubic\lumen\per\farad}
 volts to the second power lm to the third power per Fahrenheit 
\unit{\metre\squared\per\gray\cubic\lux}
 square meters per Gy lx to the third power 
\unit{\henry\second}
 H seconds 
\unit{\square\becquerel}
 Bq to the second power 
\unit{\joule\squared\per\lumen}
 joules to the second power per lm 
\unit{\cubic\lux\volt\tesla\cubed}
 lx to the third power volts tons to the third power 
\unit{\henry\tothe{5}}
 H to the fifth power 
\unit{\raiseto{4.5}\radian}
 rad raised to the 4.5 power 
\unit{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin}
 joules per mol per Kelvin 
\unit{\joule\per\mole\kelvin}
 joules per mol Kelvin 
\unit{\per\henry\tothe{5}}
 H to the negative 5 power 
\unit{\per\square\becquerel}
 Bq to the negative 2 power 
\unit{\kilogram\of{metal}}
 kilograms sub metal 
\unit[qualifier-mode = bracket]{\milli\mole\of{cat}\per\kilogram\of{prod}}
 mmol of open paren cat close paren kg of open paren prod close paren inverse 
\unit[per-mode = fraction]{\cancel\kilogram\metre\per\cancel\kilogram\per\second}
 crossed out kilograms times meters over crossed out kilograms times seconds 
\unit{\highlight{red}\kilogram\metre\per\second}
 kilograms meters per second 
\unit[unit-color = purple]{\highlight{blue}\kilogram\metre\per\second}
 kilograms meters per second 
\unit{\farad\squared\lumen\candela}
 Fahrenheit to the second power lm cd 
\unit[inter-unit-product = \cdot ]{\farad\squared\lumen\candela}
 Fahrenheit to the second power lm cd 
\unit{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin}
 joules per mol per Kelvin 
\unit{\metre\per\second\squared}
 meters seconds to the negative 2 power 
\unit[per-mode = fraction]{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin}
 joules per mol Kelvin 
\unit[per-mode = fraction]{\metre\per\second\squared}
 meters per second to the second power 
\unit{\ampere\per\mole\second}
 amperes per mol seconds 
\unit[per-mode = power-positive-first]{\ampere\per\mole\second}
 amperes seconds per mol 
\displaylines{
  \sisetup{per-mode = symbol} \\
  \unit{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin} \\
  \unit{\metre\per\second\squared} \\
  \unit[per-symbol = \ \text{div}\ ]{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin} \\
  \unit[bracket-unit-denominator = false]{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin} \\
  \sisetup{per-mode = power}
}
 5 lines Line 1: blank Line 2: joules divided by open paren mol Kelvin close paren Line 3: meters divided by seconds to the second power Line 4: joules empty div empty open paren mol Kelvin close paren Line 5: joules divided by mol Kelvin 
\unit[per-mode = repeated-symbol]{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin}
 joules divided by mol divided by Kelvin 
\displaylines{
	\sisetup{per-mode = single-symbol}
		\unit{\per\metre} \\
		\unit{\metre\per\second} \\
		\unit{\joule\per\mole\per\kelvin}
	\sisetup{per-mode = power}
}
 3 lines Line 1: reciprocal meters Line 2: meters divided by seconds Line 3: joules per mol per Kelvin 
\displaylines{
	\sisetup{per-mode = symbol}%
	\unit{\cm\cubed\per\gram} \\
	\unit[per-symbol-script-correction = ]{\cm\cubed\per\gram}
	\sisetup{per-mode = power}
}
 2 lines Line 1: cubic centimeters divided by grams Line 2: cubic centimeters divided by grams 
\unit{\pascal\per\gray\henry}
 Pa per Gy H 
\unit[sticky-per]{\pascal\per\gray\henry}
 Pa per Gy per H 
\unit{\kilogram\of{pol}\squared\per\mole\of{cat}\per\hour}
 kilograms sub pol to the second power mol sub cat to the negative 1 power per hour 
\unit[qualifier-mode = bracket]{\kilogram\of{pol}\squared\per\mole\of{cat}\per\hour}
 kg of open paren pol close paren squared of mol of open paren cat close paren inverse of reciprocal hours 
\unit[qualifier-mode = combine]{\deci\bel\of{i}}
 decibytes normal i 
\displaylines{
	\sisetup{qualifier-mode = phrase, qualifier-phrase = \ }%
	\unit{\kilogram\of{pol}\squared\per\mole\of{cat}\per\hour} \\
	\sisetup{qualifier-phrase = \ \mbox{of}\ }%
	\unit{\kilogram\of{pol}\squared\per\mole\of{cat}\per\hour}
	\sisetup{qualifier-mode = subscript, qualifier-phrase = }
}
 2 lines Line 1: kilograms empty pol squared times mol empty cat to the negative 1 power times reciprocal hours Line 2: kilograms empty of empty pol squared times mol empty of empty cat to the negative 1 power times reciprocal hours 
\unit{\Hz\tothe{0.5}}
 Hz raised to the 0.5 power 
\unit[power-half-as-sqrt]{\Hz\tothe{0.5}}
 the square root of Hz 
\unit[power-half-as-sqrt]{\Hz\tothe{-0.5}}
 Hz raised to the negative 0.5 power 
\unit{\MHz}
 MHz 
\unit[parse-units = false]{\MHz}
 
\MHz
 
\unit{\lumen}
 lm 
\unit[unit-font-command = \mathit]{\lumen}
 lm 
\unit[per-mode=power]{\kilo\gram\per\second}
 kilograms per second 
\unit[per-mode=power]{\kilo\gram\of{Fe}\per\second}
 kilograms sub Fe per second 
\unit{kg.m^2.s^{-2}}
 kilograms square meters seconds to the negative 2 power 
\unit{kg_{Fe}.m^2.s^{-2}}
 kilograms sub Fe square meters seconds to the negative 2 power 
\unit[inter-unit-product = \cdot]{\kilo\gram\meter\squared\per\second\squared}
 kilograms square meters seconds to the negative 2 power 
\unit{g}

\unit[per-mode=symbol]{\kilo\gram\meter\squared\per\second\squared}

\unit{\per\henry\tothe{5}}

\unit{\per\square\becquerel}
 Bq to the negative 2 power 
\unit{\kWh}
 kilowatts times normal h

qty

LaTeX SVG ClearSpeak 
\qty{100}{\percent}
 100 empty % 
\qty{1.23}{J.mol^{-1}.K^{-1}}
 1.23 empty joules per meter per Kelvin 
\qty{.23e7}{\candela}
 0.23 times 10 to the seventh power empty cd 
\qty[per-mode = symbol]{1.99}{\per\kilogram}
 1.99 empty 1 divided by kilograms 
\qty[per-mode = fraction]{1,345}{\coulomb\per\mole}
 1.345 empty Celsius per mol 
\qty{2.67}{\farad}
 2.67 empty Fahrenheit 
\qty[quantity-product = {\ }]{2.67}{\farad}
 2.67 empty Fahrenheit 
\qty[quantity-product = ]{2.67}{\farad}
 2.67 Fahrenheit 
\qty{1e3}{\metre\second}
 1 times 10 cubed empty meters seconds 
\qty[prefix-mode = combine-exponent]{1e3}{\metre\second}
 1 empty kilometers seconds 
\qty{10}{\kilo\gram\squared\deci\second}
 10 empty kilograms to the second power deciseconds 
\qty[prefix-mode = extract-exponent]{10}{\kilo\gram\squared\deci\second}
 10 times 10 to the negative 1 power empty kilograms to the second power seconds 
\qty[prefix-mode = extract-exponent]{7.5}{\gram}
 7.5 times 10 to the negative 3 power empty kilograms 
\qty[extract-mass-in-kilograms = false]{10}{\kilo\gram\squared\deci\second}
 10 empty kilograms to the second power deciseconds 
\qty[prefix-mode = extract-exponent, extract-mass-in-kilograms = false]{10}{\kilo\gram\squared\deci\second}
 10 times 10 to the fifth power empty grams to the second power seconds 
\qty[prefix-mode = extract-exponent, extract-mass-in-kilograms = false]{7.5}{\gram}
 7.5 empty grams 
\qty[uncertainty-mode=separate]{12.3(4)}{\kilo\gram}
 12.3 plus or minus 0.4 empty kilograms 
\qty[uncertainty-mode=separate, separate-uncertainty-units = bracket]{12.3(4)}{\kilo\gram}
 12.3 plus or minus 0.4 empty kilograms 
\qty[uncertainty-mode=separate, separate-uncertainty-units = repeat]{12.3(4)}{\kilo\gram}
 12.3 plus or minus 0.4 empty kilograms 
\qty[uncertainty-mode=separate, separate-uncertainty-units = single]{12.3(4)}{\kilo\gram}
 12.3 plus or minus 0.4 empty kilograms

\mathrm{Some\ text\ }
\qty{4}{\kilogram}
\ \mathrm{More\ text\ }
\qty[color = blue]{4}{\kilogram}
\ \mathrm{Still\ red\ here!}

numlist, qtylist, numproduct, qtyproduct, numrange, qtyrange

LaTeX SVG ClearSpeak 
\numlist{0.1;0.2;0.3}
 0.1 comma 0.2 comma and 0.3 
\numlist[list-separator = {; }]{0.1;0.2;0.3}
 0.1 semicolon 0.2 comma and 0.3 
\numlist[list-final-separator = {, }]{0.1;0.2;0.3}
 0.1 comma 0.2 comma 0.3 
\numlist[
  list-separator = { and },
  list-final-separator = { and finally }
]{0.1;0.2;0.3}
 0.1 and 0.2 open brace and finally close brace 0.3 
\numlist{0.1;0.2}
 0.1 and 0.2 
\numlist[list-pair-separator = {, and }]{0.1;0.2}
 0.1 comma and 0.2 
\numproduct{5 x 100 x 2}
 5 times 100 times 2 
\numproduct[product-symbol = \cdot]{5 x 100 x 2}
 5 times 100 times 2 
\numproduct[product-mode = phrase]{5 x 100 x 2}
 5 by 100 by 2 
\numproduct[product-mode = phrase, product-phrase = { BY }]{5 x 100 x 2}
 5 BY 100 BY 2 
\numrange{5}{100}
 5 to 100 
\numrange[range-phrase = --]{5}{100}
 5 minus minus 100 
\numlist{5e3;7e3;9e3;1e4}
 5 times 10 cubed comma 7 times 10 cubed comma 9 times 10 cubed comma and 1 times 10 to the fourth power 
\numproduct{5e3 x 7e3 x 9e3 x 1e4}
 5 times 10 cubed times 7 times 10 cubed times 9 times 10 cubed times 1 times 10 to the fourth power 
\numrange{5e3}{7e3}
 5 times 10 cubed to 7 times 10 cubed 
\displaylines{
  \sisetup{list-exponents = combine-bracket ,
    product-exponents = combine-bracket ,
    range-exponents = combine-bracket
  }
  \numlist{5e3;7e3;9e3;1e4} \\
  \numproduct{5e3 x 7e3 x 9e3 x 1e4} \\
  \numrange{5e3}{7e3} \\

  \sisetup{list-exponents = combine ,
      product-exponents = combine ,
      range-exponents = combine
  }
  \numlist{5e3;7e3;9e3;1e4} \\
  \numproduct{5e3 x 7e3 x 9e3 x 1e4} \\
  \numrange{5e3}{7e3} \\
}
6 lines Line 1: open paren 5 comma 7 comma 9 comma and 1 close paren times 10 cubed Line 2: open paren 5 times 7 times 9 times 1 close paren times 10 cubed Line 3: open paren 5 to 7 close paren times 10 cubed Line 4: 5 comma 7 comma 9 comma and 1 times 10 cubed Line 5: 5 times 7 times 9 times 1 times 10 cubed Line 6: 5 to 7 times 10 cubed 
\qtylist{2;4;6;8}{\tesla}
 2 empty tons comma 4 empty tons comma 6 empty tons comma and 8 empty tons 
\qtylist[list-units = bracket]{2;4;6;8}{\tesla}
 open paren 2 comma 4 comma 6 comma and 8 close paren empty tons 
\qtylist[list-units = repeat]{2;4;6;8}{\tesla}
 2 empty tons comma 4 empty tons comma 6 empty tons comma and 8 empty tons 
\qtylist[list-units = single]{2;4;6;8}{\tesla}
 2 comma 4 comma 6 comma and 8 empty tons 
\qtyrange{2}{4}{\degreeCelsius}
 2 empty degrees Celsius to 4 empty degrees Celsius 
\qtyrange[range-units = bracket]{2}{4}{\degreeCelsius}
 open paren 2 to 4 close paren empty degrees Celsius 
\qtyrange[range-units = repeat]{2}{4}{\degreeCelsius}
 2 empty degrees Celsius to 4 empty degrees Celsius 
\qtyrange[range-units = single]{2}{4}{\degreeCelsius}
 2 to 4 empty degrees Celsius 
\qtyproduct{2 x 4}{\metre}
 2 empty meters times 4 empty meters 
\qtyproduct[product-units = bracket-power]{2 x 4}{\metre}
 open paren 2 times 4 close paren empty square meters 
\qtyproduct[product-units = power]{2 x 4}{\metre}
 2 times 4 empty square meters 
\qtyproduct[product-units = bracket-power]{2 x 4 x 6}{\metre}
 open paren 2 times 4 times 6 close paren empty cubic meters 
\qtyproduct[product-units = bracket-power]{2 x 4}{\metre\squared}
 open paren 2 times 4 close paren empty meters to the fourth power 
\sisetup{per-mode=power}
 
\qtyrange{-729}{659}{\per\cm}
 negative 729 empty reciprocal centimeters to 659 empty per centimeter 
\qtyrange{-729}{659}{\watt\per\gram}
 negative 729 empty watts per gram to 659 empty watts per gram 
\qty{-729}{\watt\per\gram}
 negative 729 empty watts per gram 
\qtyrange{-729}{659}{\cm}
 negative 729 empty centimeters to 659 empty centimeters

 200 empty degrees Celsius to 400 empty degrees Celsius

DeclareSIUnit

LaTeX SVG ClearSpeak 
\DeclareSIUnit[inter-unit-product = \times]{\hello}{FakeUnit} \unit{\hello\kg\second}
 FakeUnit kilograms seconds 
\unit{\kg\second}
 kilograms seconds 
\unit{\hello\kg\second}
 FakeUnit kilograms seconds 
\unit[inter-unit-product = \,]{\hello\kg\second}
 FakeUnit kilograms seconds 
\DeclareSIUnit{\faren}{\degree F} \unit{\faren\kg\second}
 degrees normal F kilograms seconds 
\qty{1.2}{\faren}
 1.2 empty degrees normal F
